addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- add-cuda-kernel: Step-by-step tutorial for adding new CUDA kernels to FlashInfer
- benchmark-kernel: Guide for benchmarking FlashInfer kernels with CUPTI timing
- debug-cuda-crash: Tutorial for debugging CUDA crashes using API logging
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1

2 3 4 5 6

7

8 High-Performance GPU Kernels for Inference 9

10 11

12 | Documentation | Latest Release | Blog | Slack | Discussion Forum | 13

14 15 [![Build Status](https://ci.tlcpack.ai/job/flashinfer-ci/job/main/badge/icon)](https://ci.tlcpack.ai/job/flashinfer-ci/job/main/) 16 [![Documentation](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/actions/workflows/build-doc.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/actions/workflows/build-doc.yml) 17 18 **FlashInfer** is a library and kernel generator for inference that delivers state-of-the-art performance across diverse GPU architectures. It provides unified APIs for attention, GEMM, and MoE operations with multiple backend implementations including FlashAttention-2/3, cuDNN, CUTLASS, and TensorRT-LLM. 19 20 ## Why FlashInfer? 21 22 - **State-of-the-art Performance**: Optimized kernels for prefill, decode, and mixed batching scenarios 23 - **Multiple Backends**: Automatically selects the best backend for your hardware and workload 24 - **Modern Architecture Support**: Support for SM75 (Turing) and later (through Blackwell) 25 - **Low-Precision Compute**: FP8 and FP4 quantization for attention, GEMM, and MoE operations 26 - **Production-Ready**: CUDAGraph and torch.compile compatible for low-latency serving 27 28 ## Core Features 29 30 ### Attention Kernels 31 - **Paged and Ragged KV-Cache**: Efficient memory management for dynamic batch serving 32 - **Decode, Prefill, and Append**: Optimized kernels for all attention phases 33 - **MLA Attention**: Native support for DeepSeek's Multi-Latent Attention 34 - **Cascade Attention**: Memory-efficient hierarchical KV-Cache for shared prefixes 35 - **Sparse Attention**: Block-sparse and variable block-sparse patterns 36 - **POD-Attention**: Fused prefill+decode for mixed batching 37 38 ### GEMM & Linear Operations 39 - **BF16 GEMM**: BF16 matrix multiplication for SM10.0+ GPUs. 40 - **FP8 GEMM**: Per-tensor and groupwise scaling 41 - **FP4 GEMM**: NVFP4 and MXFP4 matrix multiplication for Blackwell GPUs 42 - **Grouped GEMM**: Efficient batched matrix operations for LoRA and multi-expert routing 43 44 ### Mixture of Experts (MoE) 45 - **Fused MoE Kernels** 46 - **Multiple Routing Methods**: DeepSeek-V3, Llama-4, and standard top-k routing 47 - **Quantized MoE**: FP8 and FP4 expert weights with block-wise scaling 48 49 ### Sampling & Decoding 50 - **Sorting-Free Sampling**: Efficient Top-K, Top-P, and Min-P without sorting 51 - **Speculative Decoding**: Chain speculative sampling support 52 53 ### Communication 54 - **AllReduce**: Custom implementations 55 - **Multi-Node NVLink**: MNNVL support for multi-node inference 56 - **NVSHMEM Integration**: For distributed memory operations 57 58 ### Other Operators 59 - **RoPE**: LLaMA-style rotary position embeddings (including LLaMA 3.1) 60 - **Normalization**: RMSNorm, LayerNorm, Gemma-style fused operations 61 - **Activations**: SiLU, GELU with fused gating 62 63 ## GPU Support 64 65 | Architecture | Compute Capability | Example GPUs | 66 |--------------|-------------------|------| 67 | Turing | SM 7.5 | T4, RTX 20 series | 68 | Ampere | SM 8.0, 8.6 | A100, A10, RTX 30 series | 69 | Ada Lovelace | SM 8.9 | L4, L40, RTX 40 series | 70 | Hopper | SM 9.0 | H100, H200 | 71 | Blackwell | SM 10.0, 10.3 | B200, B300 | 72 | Blackwell | SM 11.0 | Jetson Thor | 73 | Blackwell | SM 12.0, 12.1 | RTX 50 series, DGX Spark | 74 75 > **Note:** Not all features are supported across all compute capabilities. 76 77 ## News 78 79 Latest: [![GitHub Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer)](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/releases/latest) 80 81 Notable updates: 82 - [2025-10-08] Blackwell support added in [v0.4.0](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/releases/tag/v0.4.0) 83 - [2025-03-10] [Blog Post](https://flashinfer.ai/2025/03/10/sampling.html) Sorting-Free GPU Kernels for LLM Sampling, which explains the design of sampling kernels in FlashInfer. 84 85 ## Getting Started 86 87 ### Installation 88 89 **Quickstart:** 90 91 ```bash 92 pip install flashinfer-python 93 ``` 94 95 **Package Options:** 96 97 - **flashinfer-python**: Core package that compiles/downloads kernels on first use 98 - **flashinfer-cubin**: Pre-compiled kernel binaries for all supported GPU architectures 99 - **flashinfer-jit-cache**: Pre-built kernel cache for specific CUDA versions 100 101 **For faster initialization and offline usage**, install the optional packages to have most kernels pre-compiled: 102 103 ```bash 104 pip install flashinfer-python flashinfer-cubin 105 # JIT cache (replace cu129 with your CUDA version) 106 pip install flashinfer-jit-cache --index-url https://flashinfer.ai/whl/cu129 107 ``` 108 109 **For Blackwell (SM100+) CuTe DSL kernels**, install with the CUDA 13 extra to enable Blackwell-optimized kernels: 110 111 ```bash 112 pip install flashinfer-python[cu13] 113 ``` 114 115 ### Verify Installation 116 117 ```bash 118 flashinfer show-config 119 ``` 120 121 ### Basic Usage 122 123 ```python 124 import torch 125 import flashinfer 126 127 # Single decode attention 128 q = torch.randn(32, 128, device="cuda", dtype=torch.float16) # [num_qo_heads, head_dim] 129 k = torch.randn(2048, 32, 128, device="cuda", dtype=torch.float16) # [kv_len, num_kv_heads, head_dim] 130 v = torch.randn(2048, 32, 128, device="cuda", dtype=torch.float16) 131 132 output = flashinfer.single_decode_with_kv_cache(q, k, v) 133 ``` 134 135 See [documentation](https://docs.flashinfer.ai/) for comprehensive API reference and tutorials. 136 137 ### Install from Source 138 139 ```bash 140 git clone https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer.git --recursive 141 cd flashinfer 142 python -m pip install -v . 143 ``` 144 145 **For development**, install in editable mode: 146 147 ```bash 148 python -m pip install --no-build-isolation -e . -v 149 ``` 150 151 > **Note:** When using `--no-build-isolation`, pip does not automatically install build dependencies. FlashInfer requires `setuptools>=77`. If you encounter an error like `AttributeError: module 'setuptools.build_meta' has no attribute 'prepare_metadata_for_build_editable'`, upgrade pip and setuptools first: 152 > ```bash 153 > python -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools 154 > ``` 155 156 Build optional packages: 157 158 ```bash 159 # flashinfer-cubin 160 cd flashinfer-cubin 161 python -m build --no-isolation --wheel 162 python -m pip install dist/*.whl 163 ``` 164 165 ```bash 166 # flashinfer-jit-cache (customize for your target GPUs) 167 export FLASHINFER_CUDA_ARCH_LIST="7.5 8.0 8.9 9.0a 10.0a 10.3a 11.0a 12.0f" 168 cd flashinfer-jit-cache 169 python -m build --no-isolation --wheel 170 python -m pip install dist/*.whl 171 ``` 172 173 For more details, see the [Install from Source documentation](https://docs.flashinfer.ai/installation.html#install-from-source). 174 175 ### Nightly Builds 176 177 ```bash 178 pip install -U --pre flashinfer-python --index-url https://flashinfer.ai/whl/nightly/ --no-deps 179 pip install flashinfer-python # Install dependencies from PyPI 180 pip install -U --pre flashinfer-cubin --index-url https://flashinfer.ai/whl/nightly/ 181 # JIT cache (replace cu129 with your CUDA version) 182 pip install -U --pre flashinfer-jit-cache --index-url https://flashinfer.ai/whl/nightly/cu129 183 ``` 184 185 ### CLI Tools 186 187 FlashInfer provides several CLI commands for configuration, module management, and development: 188 189 ```bash 190 # Verify installation and view configuration 191 flashinfer show-config 192 193 # List and inspect modules 194 flashinfer list-modules 195 flashinfer module-status 196 197 # Manage artifacts and cache 198 flashinfer download-cubin 199 flashinfer clear-cache 200 201 # For developers: generate compile_commands.json for IDE integration 202 flashinfer export-compile-commands [output_path] 203 ``` 204 205 For complete documentation, see the [CLI reference](https://docs.flashinfer.ai/cli.html). 206 207 ## API Logging 208 209 FlashInfer provides comprehensive API logging for debugging. Enable it using environment variables: 210 211 ```bash 212 # Enable logging (levels: 0=off (default), 1=basic, 3=detailed, 5=statistics) 213 export FLASHINFER_LOGLEVEL=3 214 215 # Set log destination (stdout (default), stderr, or file path) 216 export FLASHINFER_LOGDEST=stdout 217 ``` 218 219 For detailed information about logging levels, configuration, and advanced features, see [Logging](https://docs.flashinfer.ai/logging.html) in our documentation. 220 221 ## Custom Attention Variants 222 223 Users can customize their own attention variants with additional parameters. For more details, refer to our [JIT examples](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/blob/main/tests/utils/test_jit_example.py). 224 225 ## CUDA Support 226 227 **Supported CUDA Versions:** 12.6, 12.8, 13.0, 13.1 228 229 > **Note:** FlashInfer strives to follow PyTorch's supported CUDA versions plus the latest CUDA release. 230 231 ## Adoption 232 233 FlashInfer powers inference in: 234 235 - [SGLang](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang) 236 - [vLLM](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm) 237 - [TensorRT-LLM](https://github.com/NVIDIA/TensorRT-LLM) 238 - [TGI (Text Generation Inference)](https://github.com/huggingface/text-generation-inference) 239 - [MLC-LLM](https://github.com/mlc-ai/mlc-llm) 240 - [LightLLM](https://github.com/ModelTC/lightllm) 241 - [lorax](https://github.com/predibase/lorax) 242 - [ScaleLLM](https://github.com/vectorch-ai/ScaleLLM) 243 244 ## Acknowledgement 245 246 FlashInfer is inspired by [FlashAttention](https://github.com/dao-AILab/flash-attention/), [vLLM](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm), [stream-K](https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.03598), [CUTLASS](https://github.com/nvidia/cutlass), and [AITemplate](https://github.com/facebookincubator/AITemplate). 247 248 ## Citation 249 250 If you find FlashInfer helpful in your project or research, please consider citing our [paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.01005): 251 252 ```bibtex 253 @article{ye2025flashinfer, 254 title = {FlashInfer: Efficient and Customizable Attention Engine for LLM Inference Serving}, 255 author = { 256 Ye, Zihao and 257 Chen, Lequn and 258 Lai, Ruihang and 259 Lin, Wuwei and 260 Zhang, Yineng and 261 Wang, Stephanie and 262 Chen, Tianqi and 263 Kasikci, Baris and 264 Grover, Vinod and 265 Krishnamurthy, Arvind and 266 Ceze, Luis 267 }, 268 journal = {arXiv preprint arXiv:2501.01005}, 269 year = {2025}, 270 url = {https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.01005} 271 } 272 ``` 273

High-Performance GPU Kernels for Inference

| Documentation | Latest Release | Blog | Slack | Discussion Forum |

[![Build Status](https://ci.tlcpack.ai/job/flashinfer-ci/job/main/badge/icon)](https://ci.tlcpack.ai/job/flashinfer-ci/job/main/) [![Documentation](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/actions/workflows/build-doc.yml/badge.svg)](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/actions/workflows/build-doc.yml) **FlashInfer** is a library and kernel generator for inference that delivers state-of-the-art performance across diverse GPU architectures. It provides unified APIs for attention, GEMM, and MoE operations with multiple backend implementations including FlashAttention-2/3, cuDNN, CUTLASS, and TensorRT-LLM. ## Why FlashInfer? - **State-of-the-art Performance**: Optimized kernels for prefill, decode, and mixed batching scenarios - **Multiple Backends**: Automatically selects the best backend for your hardware and workload - **Modern Architecture Support**: Support for SM75 (Turing) and later (through Blackwell) - **Low-Precision Compute**: FP8 and FP4 quantization for attention, GEMM, and MoE operations - **Production-Ready**: CUDAGraph and torch.compile compatible for low-latency serving ## Core Features ### Attention Kernels - **Paged and Ragged KV-Cache**: Efficient memory management for dynamic batch serving - **Decode, Prefill, and Append**: Optimized kernels for all attention phases - **MLA Attention**: Native support for DeepSeek's Multi-Latent Attention - **Cascade Attention**: Memory-efficient hierarchical KV-Cache for shared prefixes - **Sparse Attention**: Block-sparse and variable block-sparse patterns - **POD-Attention**: Fused prefill+decode for mixed batching ### GEMM & Linear Operations - **BF16 GEMM**: BF16 matrix multiplication for SM10.0+ GPUs. - **FP8 GEMM**: Per-tensor and groupwise scaling - **FP4 GEMM**: NVFP4 and MXFP4 matrix multiplication for Blackwell GPUs - **Grouped GEMM**: Efficient batched matrix operations for LoRA and multi-expert routing ### Mixture of Experts (MoE) - **Fused MoE Kernels** - **Multiple Routing Methods**: DeepSeek-V3, Llama-4, and standard top-k routing - **Quantized MoE**: FP8 and FP4 expert weights with block-wise scaling ### Sampling & Decoding - **Sorting-Free Sampling**: Efficient Top-K, Top-P, and Min-P without sorting - **Speculative Decoding**: Chain speculative sampling support ### Communication - **AllReduce**: Custom implementations - **Multi-Node NVLink**: MNNVL support for multi-node inference - **NVSHMEM Integration**: For distributed memory operations ### Other Operators - **RoPE**: LLaMA-style rotary position embeddings (including LLaMA 3.1) - **Normalization**: RMSNorm, LayerNorm, Gemma-style fused operations - **Activations**: SiLU, GELU with fused gating ## GPU Support | Architecture | Compute Capability | Example GPUs | |--------------|-------------------|------| | Turing | SM 7.5 | T4, RTX 20 series | | Ampere | SM 8.0, 8.6 | A100, A10, RTX 30 series | | Ada Lovelace | SM 8.9 | L4, L40, RTX 40 series | | Hopper | SM 9.0 | H100, H200 | | Blackwell | SM 10.0, 10.3 | B200, B300 | | Blackwell | SM 11.0 | Jetson Thor | | Blackwell | SM 12.0, 12.1 | RTX 50 series, DGX Spark | > **Note:** Not all features are supported across all compute capabilities. ## News Latest: [![GitHub Release](https://img.shields.io/github/v/release/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer)](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/releases/latest) Notable updates: - [2025-10-08] Blackwell support added in [v0.4.0](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/releases/tag/v0.4.0) - [2025-03-10] [Blog Post](https://flashinfer.ai/2025/03/10/sampling.html) Sorting-Free GPU Kernels for LLM Sampling, which explains the design of sampling kernels in FlashInfer. ## Getting Started ### Installation **Quickstart:** ```bash pip install flashinfer-python ``` **Package Options:** - **flashinfer-python**: Core package that compiles/downloads kernels on first use - **flashinfer-cubin**: Pre-compiled kernel binaries for all supported GPU architectures - **flashinfer-jit-cache**: Pre-built kernel cache for specific CUDA versions **For faster initialization and offline usage**, install the optional packages to have most kernels pre-compiled: ```bash pip install flashinfer-python flashinfer-cubin # JIT cache (replace cu129 with your CUDA version) pip install flashinfer-jit-cache --index-url https://flashinfer.ai/whl/cu129 ``` **For Blackwell (SM100+) CuTe DSL kernels**, install with the CUDA 13 extra to enable Blackwell-optimized kernels: ```bash pip install flashinfer-python[cu13] ``` ### Verify Installation ```bash flashinfer show-config ``` ### Basic Usage ```python import torch import flashinfer # Single decode attention q = torch.randn(32, 128, device="cuda", dtype=torch.float16) # [num_qo_heads, head_dim] k = torch.randn(2048, 32, 128, device="cuda", dtype=torch.float16) # [kv_len, num_kv_heads, head_dim] v = torch.randn(2048, 32, 128, device="cuda", dtype=torch.float16) output = flashinfer.single_decode_with_kv_cache(q, k, v) ``` See [documentation](https://docs.flashinfer.ai/) for comprehensive API reference and tutorials. ### Install from Source ```bash git clone https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer.git --recursive cd flashinfer python -m pip install -v . ``` **For development**, install in editable mode: ```bash python -m pip install --no-build-isolation -e . -v ``` > **Note:** When using `--no-build-isolation`, pip does not automatically install build dependencies. FlashInfer requires `setuptools>=77`. If you encounter an error like `AttributeError: module 'setuptools.build_meta' has no attribute 'prepare_metadata_for_build_editable'`, upgrade pip and setuptools first: > ```bash > python -m pip install --upgrade pip setuptools > ``` Build optional packages: ```bash # flashinfer-cubin cd flashinfer-cubin python -m build --no-isolation --wheel python -m pip install dist/*.whl ``` ```bash # flashinfer-jit-cache (customize for your target GPUs) export FLASHINFER_CUDA_ARCH_LIST="7.5 8.0 8.9 9.0a 10.0a 10.3a 11.0a 12.0f" cd flashinfer-jit-cache python -m build --no-isolation --wheel python -m pip install dist/*.whl ``` For more details, see the [Install from Source documentation](https://docs.flashinfer.ai/installation.html#install-from-source). ### Nightly Builds ```bash pip install -U --pre flashinfer-python --index-url https://flashinfer.ai/whl/nightly/ --no-deps pip install flashinfer-python # Install dependencies from PyPI pip install -U --pre flashinfer-cubin --index-url https://flashinfer.ai/whl/nightly/ # JIT cache (replace cu129 with your CUDA version) pip install -U --pre flashinfer-jit-cache --index-url https://flashinfer.ai/whl/nightly/cu129 ``` ### CLI Tools FlashInfer provides several CLI commands for configuration, module management, and development: ```bash # Verify installation and view configuration flashinfer show-config # List and inspect modules flashinfer list-modules flashinfer module-status # Manage artifacts and cache flashinfer download-cubin flashinfer clear-cache # For developers: generate compile_commands.json for IDE integration flashinfer export-compile-commands [output_path] ``` For complete documentation, see the [CLI reference](https://docs.flashinfer.ai/cli.html). ## API Logging FlashInfer provides comprehensive API logging for debugging. Enable it using environment variables: ```bash # Enable logging (levels: 0=off (default), 1=basic, 3=detailed, 5=statistics) export FLASHINFER_LOGLEVEL=3 # Set log destination (stdout (default), stderr, or file path) export FLASHINFER_LOGDEST=stdout ``` For detailed information about logging levels, configuration, and advanced features, see [Logging](https://docs.flashinfer.ai/logging.html) in our documentation. ## Custom Attention Variants Users can customize their own attention variants with additional parameters. For more details, refer to our [JIT examples](https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/blob/main/tests/utils/test_jit_example.py). ## CUDA Support **Supported CUDA Versions:** 12.6, 12.8, 13.0, 13.1 > **Note:** FlashInfer strives to follow PyTorch's supported CUDA versions plus the latest CUDA release. ## Adoption FlashInfer powers inference in: - [SGLang](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang) - [vLLM](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm) - [TensorRT-LLM](https://github.com/NVIDIA/TensorRT-LLM) - [TGI (Text Generation Inference)](https://github.com/huggingface/text-generation-inference) - [MLC-LLM](https://github.com/mlc-ai/mlc-llm) - [LightLLM](https://github.com/ModelTC/lightllm) - [lorax](https://github.com/predibase/lorax) - [ScaleLLM](https://github.com/vectorch-ai/ScaleLLM) ## Acknowledgement FlashInfer is inspired by [FlashAttention](https://github.com/dao-AILab/flash-attention/), [vLLM](https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm), [stream-K](https://arxiv.org/abs/2301.03598), [CUTLASS](https://github.com/nvidia/cutlass), and [AITemplate](https://github.com/facebookincubator/AITemplate). ## Citation If you find FlashInfer helpful in your project or research, please consider citing our [paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.01005): ```bibtex @article{ye2025flashinfer, title = {FlashInfer: Efficient and Customizable Attention Engine for LLM Inference Serving}, author = { Ye, Zihao and Chen, Lequn and Lai, Ruihang and Lin, Wuwei and Zhang, Yineng and Wang, Stephanie and Chen, Tianqi and Kasikci, Baris and Grover, Vinod and Krishnamurthy, Arvind and Ceze, Luis }, journal = {arXiv preprint arXiv:2501.01005}, year = {2025}, url = {https://arxiv.org/abs/2501.01005} } ```

=== pyproject ===
# Copyright (c) 2024 by FlashInfer team.
#
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#   http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.

[project]
name = "flashinfer-python"
dynamic = ["version", "dependencies"]
description = "FlashInfer: Kernel Library for LLM Serving"
requires-python = ">=3.10,<4.0"
authors = [{ name = "FlashInfer team" }]
license = "Apache-2.0"
readme = "README.md"
urls = { Homepage = "https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer" }
license-files = ["LICENSE", "LICENSE*.txt"]

[project.optional-dependencies]
cu12 = ["nvidia-cutlass-dsl>=4.5.0"]
cu13 = ["nvidia-cutlass-dsl[cu13]>=4.5.0"]
# DEPRECATED alias, kept so existing `pip install ".[nvep]"` commands keep
# working. The moe_ep runtime deps (cuda-python, nccl4py) are now part of the
# BASE dependencies (requirements.txt) and the NIXL-EP submodule build runs by
# default (best-effort) on `pip install .` — see the moe_ep section at the top
# of build_backend.py. libnccl comes from torch's own nvidia-nccl-cu13 pin;
# the >=2.30.7 B200 floor is enforced at runtime (moe_ep/_validators.py) and
# upgraded --no-deps by the build hook on source installs. Opt out of the
# native build with BUILD_NVEP=0 (or BUILD_NIXL_EP=0 / BUILD_NCCL_EP=0).
nvep = []

[project.scripts]
flashinfer = "flashinfer.__main__:cli"

[build-system]
requires = ["setuptools>=77", "packaging>=24", "apache-tvm-ffi>=0.1.6,!=0.1.8,!=0.1.8.post0,<0.2"]
build-backend = "build_backend"
backend-path = ["."]

[tool.codespell]
ignore-words-list = "3nd"
skip = ["build", "3rdparty", "dist", ".venv"]

[tool.setuptools]
include-package-data = false
py-modules = ["build_backend", "build_utils"]

[tool.setuptools.dynamic]
version = { attr = "flashinfer._build_meta.__version__" }
dependencies = { file = ["requirements.txt"] }

[tool.setuptools.packages.find]
where = ["."]
include = ["flashinfer*"]
exclude = ["flashinfer-jit-cache*", "flashinfer-cubin*"]

[tool.setuptools.package-dir]
"flashinfer.data" = "."
"flashinfer.data.cutlass" = "3rdparty/cutlass"
"flashinfer.data.spdlog" = "3rdparty/spdlog"
"flashinfer.data.cccl" = "3rdparty/cccl"

[tool.setuptools.package-data]
"flashinfer" = ["_build_meta.py"]
# JIT sources for the blk64 BSA kernel extension (compiled on-demand via
# torch.utils.cpp_extension.load in flashinfer/cute_dsl/sparse/blk64/loader.py).
# These live inside the package tree rather than csrc/, so they must be
# registered explicitly or they won't be included in non-editable installs.
"flashinfer.cute_dsl.sparse.blk64" = [
  "*.cpp",
  "*.cu",
  "*.h",
  "*.hpp",
=== version ===
0.6.14

import dataclasses
import functools
import logging
import os
from contextlib import nullcontext
from datetime import datetime
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Dict, List, Optional, Sequence, Union, Hashable

import tvm_ffi
from filelock import FileLock

from ..compilation_context import CompilationContext
from . import env as jit_env
from .cpp_ext import generate_ninja_build_for_op, get_nvcc_parallelism_flags, run_ninja
from .utils import write_if_different

os.makedirs(jit_env.FLASHINFER_WORKSPACE_DIR, exist_ok=True)
# Note: Do NOT create FLASHINFER_CSRC_DIR here - it's the package directory
# which may be read-only after installation


class MissingJITCacheError(RuntimeError):
    """
    Exception raised when JIT compilation is disabled and the JIT cache
    does not contain the required precompiled module.

    This error indicates that a module needs to be added to the JIT cache
    build configuration.

    Attributes:
        spec: JitSpec of the missing module
        message: Error message
    """

    def __init__(self, message: str, spec: Optional["JitSpec"] = None):
        self.spec = spec
        super().__init__(message)


class FlashInferJITLogger(logging.Logger):
    def __init__(self, name):
        super().__init__(name)
        logging_level = os.getenv("FLASHINFER_LOGGING_LEVEL", "info")
        self.setLevel(logging_level.upper())
        self.addHandler(logging.StreamHandler())
        log_path = jit_env.FLASHINFER_WORKSPACE_DIR / "flashinfer_jit.log"
        if not os.path.exists(log_path):
            # create an empty file
            with open(log_path, "w") as f:  # noqa: F841
                pass
        self.addHandler(logging.FileHandler(log_path))
        # set the format of the log
        self.handlers[0].setFormatter(
            logging.Formatter(
                "%(asctime)s - %(levelname)s - %(filename)s:%(lineno)d - flashinfer.jit: %(message)s"
            )
        )
        self.handlers[1].setFormatter(
            logging.Formatter(

## FlashInfer ATTENTION Subsystem Map

### 1. `flashinfer/attention/` Python Package

**Files:**
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/attention/__init__.py` - Exports `BatchAttention` and `BatchAttentionWithAttentionSinkWrapper`
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/attention/_core.py` - Core `BatchAttention` wrapper class with `plan()`/`run()` pattern
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/attention/cute_dsl/__init__.py` - CuTe DSL attention module
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/attention/cute_dsl/fmha.py` - FMHA CuTe DSL implementations

**Main Wrapper Classes:**
- **`BatchAttention`** - Holistic batched attention wrapper (fuses paged-prefill + paged-decode), dispatches per-request based on qo_indptr ranges
- **`BatchAttentionWithAttentionSinkWrapper`** - Extends `BatchPrefillWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`, adds attention sink support
- **`BatchDecodeWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`** (in `decode.py`) - Batch decode with paged KV-cache
- **`BatchPrefillWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`** (in `prefill.py`) - Batch prefill with paged KV-cache
- **`BatchMLAWrapper`** (in `mla/_core.py`) - Multi-head Latent Attention wrapper

**Plan/Run Pattern:**
- `plan(shape_params)` - Analyzes batch structure, creates auxiliary data structures, returns plan_info
- `run(q, kv_cache, ...)` - Executes kernel using cached plan_info, reuses workspace buffers

### 2. Main Attention Modules

**`flashinfer/decode.py`** - Batch/single decode attention, MLA decode, cuDNN decode, XQA decode functions
- Entry wrappers: `BatchDecodeWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`, `BatchDecodeMLAWrapper`
- Functions: `single_decode_with_kv_cache()`, `batch_decode_with_kv_cache()`

**`flashinfer/prefill.py`** - Batch/single prefill attention with paged/ragged KV-cache
- Entry wrappers: `BatchPrefillWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`, `BatchPrefillWithRaggedKVCacheWrapper`
- Functions: `single_prefill_with_kv_cache()`, `batch_prefill_with_kv_cache()`

**`flashinfer/cascade.py`** - Cascade attention for recursive state merging
- Functions: `merge_state()`, `merge_states()`, `merge_state_in_place()`
- Module: `gen_cascade_module()`

**`flashinfer/pod.py`** - POD (Position-Output Decoupled) Attention wrapper
- Wrapper: `PODWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`, `BatchPODWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`
- Modules: `gen_pod_module()`, `gen_batch_pod_module()`

**`flashinfer/sparse.py`** - Block sparse attention (BSR to VSA conversion)
- Functions: `block_sparse_attention()`, `variable_block_sparse_attention()`
- Uses custom Q2K indexing patterns

**`flashinfer/xqa.py`** - XQA (Cross-Query Attention) module
- Functions: `xqa()`, `xqa_mla()`
- JIT generators: `gen_xqa_module()`, `gen_xqa_module_mla()`

**`flashinfer/kda_decode.py`** - Key-Driven Attention decode (recurrent KDA)
- Functions: `recurrent_kda()`
- Backend: `flashinfer.kda_kernels.recurrent_kda`

**`flashinfer/mla/`** - Multi-head Latent Attention
- `_core.py` - Core MLA wrapper (`BatchMLAWrapper`), `determine_mla_backend()` dispatch
- `_sparse_mla_sm120.py` - SM120 sparse MLA implementation

- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/csrc/batch_attention_customize_config.jinja` - Holistic attention config
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/csrc/batch_mla_config.jinja` - MLA kernel configuration
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/csrc/batch_*_kernel_inst.jinja` - Kernel instantiation templates
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/csrc/pod_*.jinja` - POD kernel templates

---

### 5. `flashinfer/jit/attention/` Module Generators

**Files:**
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/jit/attention/modules.py` - Main JIT generator functions, URI generators
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/jit/attention/variants.py` - Attention variant declarations (fa2/fa3)
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/jit/attention/utils.py` - JIT utilities, additional params generation
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/jit/attention/fmha_v2/fmha_library.py` - FMHA v2 kernel library
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/jit/attention/fmha_v2/generate_kernels.py` - FMHA v2 kernel enumeration
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/jit/attention/fmha_v2/generator_utils.py` - FMHA v2 generation utilities

**Generator Functions:**
- `gen_batch_attention_module()`, `gen_batch_decode_module()`, `gen_batch_prefill_module()`
- `gen_single_decode_module()`, `gen_single_prefill_module()`
- `gen_batch_mla_module()`, `gen_pod_module()`, `gen_fmha_v2_module()`
- `gen_cudnn_fmha_module()`, `gen_trtllm_gen_fmha_module()`

---

### 6. Backend Selection Mechanism

**Backend Dispatch:**
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/utils.py` - `determine_attention_backend()` function (line 483), `backend_requirement` decorator (line 1013)

**Backends:**
- **FlashAttention2 (fa2)** - CUDA cores, sm80+
- **FlashAttention3 (fa3)** - Tensor cores (Hopper sm90), CUTLASS-based
- **cuDNN** - `flashinfer/cudnn/` package with `cudnn_batch_decode_with_kv_cache()`, `cudnn_batch_prefill_with_kv_cache()`
- **TensorRT-LLM (trtllm-gen)** - `flashinfer/trtllm_utils.py`, `gen_trtllm_gen_fmha_module()`
- **CUTLASS** - `gen_fmha_cutlass_sm100a_module()`, CUTLASS MLA kernels

**Backend Selection Logic:**
```python
def determine_attention_backend(device, pos_encoding_mode, use_fp16_qk_reductions,
                                 use_custom_mask, dtype_q, dtype_kv, *,
                                 head_dim_qk=None, head_dim_vo=None) -> str:
    # Returns "fa2", "fa3", "cudnn", or "trtllm-gen" based on:
    # - GPU compute capability (sm80, sm90, sm100, sm120)
    # - Data types (fp16, bf16, fp8, nvfp4)
    # - Head dimensions
    # - Custom mask requirements
```

**Backend Files:**
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/cudnn/decode.py` - cuDNN decode backend
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/cudnn/prefill.py` - cuDNN prefill backend
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/csrc/cudnn_sdpa_kernel_launcher.cu` - cuDNN SDPA launcher
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/trtllm_utils.py` - TensorRT-LLM utilities

---

### Data Flow: Python API -> JIT Gen -> csrc Launcher -> Include Kernel

1. **Python API** (`flashinfer/decode.py`, `flashinfer/prefill.py`)
   - Wrapper class `plan()` analyzes batch structure, calls JIT module's `plan()`
   - Wrapper class `run()` invokes JIT module's `run()` with plan_info

2. **JIT Generation** (`flashinfer/jit/attention/modules.py`)
   - `gen_*_module()` functions render Jinja templates to generate `.cu` and `.inc` files
   - Templates: `*_customize_config.jinja`, `*_kernel_inst.jinja`
   - Compiled via NVRTC to shared library

3. **csrc Launcher** (`csrc/batch_*_jit_binding.cu`)
   - `TVM_FFI_DLL_EXPORT_TYPED_FUNC(plan, ...)` - exported plan function
   - `TVM_FFI_DLL_EXPORT_TYPED_FUNC(run, ...)` - exported run function
   - Calls template-generated kernel functions

4. **Include Kernel** (`include/flashinfer/attention/*.cuh`)
   - `decode.cuh`, `prefill.cuh`, `scheduler.cuh` contain actual CUDA kernels
   - `DISPATCH_*` macros specialize templates for dtypes, head dims, variants

---

This map provides the complete structural overview of FlashInfer's attention subsystem with concrete file paths and their roles in the learning handbook design.</result>

"""
Copyright (c) 2024 by FlashInfer team.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");

See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
"""

# NOTE(lequn): Do not "from .jit.env import xxx".
# Do "from .jit import env as jit_env" and use "jit_env.xxx" instead.
# This helps AOT script to override envs.

import os
import pathlib
from ..compilation_context import CompilationContext
from ..version import __version__ as flashinfer_version


def has_flashinfer_jit_cache() -> bool:
    """
    Check if flashinfer_jit_cache module is available.

    Returns:
        True if flashinfer_jit_cache exists, False otherwise
    """
    import importlib.util

    return importlib.util.find_spec("flashinfer_jit_cache") is not None


def has_flashinfer_cubin() -> bool:
    """
    Check if flashinfer_cubin module is available.

    Returns:
        True if flashinfer_cubin exists, False otherwise
    """
    import importlib.util

    return importlib.util.find_spec("flashinfer_cubin") is not None


FLASHINFER_BASE_DIR: pathlib.Path = pathlib.Path(
    os.getenv("FLASHINFER_WORKSPACE_BASE", pathlib.Path.home().as_posix())
)

FLASHINFER_CACHE_DIR: pathlib.Path = FLASHINFER_BASE_DIR / ".cache" / "flashinfer"
_package_root: pathlib.Path = pathlib.Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]


def _get_cubin_dir():
    """
    Get the cubin directory path with the following priority:
    1. flashinfer-cubin package if installed
    2. Environment variable FLASHINFER_CUBIN_DIR
    3. Default cache directory
    """
    # First check if flashinfer-cubin package is installed
    if has_flashinfer_cubin():
        import flashinfer_cubin

        flashinfer_cubin_version = flashinfer_cubin.__version__
        # Allow bypassing version check with environment variable
        # NOTE(yiyang): skip version check for editable/source installs where
        # flashinfer_version falls back to "0.0.0+unknown" (no _build_meta.py).
        if (
            not os.getenv("FLASHINFER_DISABLE_VERSION_CHECK")
            and flashinfer_version != "0.0.0+unknown"
            and flashinfer_version != flashinfer_cubin_version
        ):
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"flashinfer-cubin version ({flashinfer_cubin_version}) does not match "
                f"flashinfer version ({flashinfer_version}). "
                "Please install the same version of both packages. "
                "Set FLASHINFER_DISABLE_VERSION_CHECK=1 to bypass this check."
            )

        return pathlib.Path(flashinfer_cubin.get_cubin_dir())

    # Then check environment variable
    env_dir = os.getenv("FLASHINFER_CUBIN_DIR")
    if env_dir:
        return pathlib.Path(env_dir)

    # Fall back to default cache directory
    return FLASHINFER_CACHE_DIR / "cubins"


FLASHINFER_CUBIN_DIR: pathlib.Path = _get_cubin_dir()


def _get_aot_dir():
    """
    Get the AOT directory path with the following priority:
    1. flashinfer-jit-cache package if installed
    2. Default fallback to _package_root / "data" / "aot"
    """
    # First check if flashinfer-jit-cache package is installed
    if has_flashinfer_jit_cache():
        import flashinfer_jit_cache

        flashinfer_jit_cache_version = flashinfer_jit_cache.__version__
        # NOTE(Zihao): we don't use exact version match here because the version of flashinfer-jit-cache
        # contains the CUDA version suffix: e.g. 0.3.1+cu129.
        # Allow bypassing version check with environment variable
        if (
            not os.getenv("FLASHINFER_DISABLE_VERSION_CHECK")
            and flashinfer_version != "0.0.0+unknown"
            and not flashinfer_jit_cache_version.startswith(flashinfer_version)
        ):
            raise RuntimeError(
                f"flashinfer-jit-cache version ({flashinfer_jit_cache_version}) does not match "
                f"flashinfer version ({flashinfer_version}). "
                "Please install the same version of both packages. "
                "Set FLASHINFER_DISABLE_VERSION_CHECK=1 to bypass this check."
            )

        return pathlib.Path(flashinfer_jit_cache.get_jit_cache_dir())

    # Fall back to default directory
    return _package_root / "data" / "aot"


FLASHINFER_AOT_DIR: pathlib.Path = _get_aot_dir()


def _get_workspace_dir_name() -> pathlib.Path:
    compilation_context = CompilationContext()
    # NOTE(Zihao): sorted() is crucial here to ensure deterministic directory names.
    # Without it, the same set of CUDA archs could generate different directory names
    # across runs (e.g., "75_80_89" vs "89_75_80"), causing cache fragmentation.
    arch = "_".join(
        f"{major}{minor}"
        for major, minor in sorted(compilation_context.TARGET_CUDA_ARCHS)
    )
    return FLASHINFER_CACHE_DIR / flashinfer_version / arch


# use pathlib
FLASHINFER_WORKSPACE_DIR: pathlib.Path = _get_workspace_dir_name()
FLASHINFER_JIT_DIR: pathlib.Path = FLASHINFER_WORKSPACE_DIR / "cached_ops"
FLASHINFER_GEN_SRC_DIR: pathlib.Path = FLASHINFER_WORKSPACE_DIR / "generated"
FLASHINFER_DATA: pathlib.Path = _package_root / "data"
FLASHINFER_INCLUDE_DIR: pathlib.Path = _package_root / "data" / "include"
FLASHINFER_CSRC_DIR: pathlib.Path = _package_root / "data" / "csrc"
# FLASHINFER_SRC_DIR = _package_root / "data" / "src"
CUTLASS_INCLUDE_DIRS: list[pathlib.Path] = [
    _package_root / "data" / "cutlass" / "include",
    _package_root / "data" / "cutlass" / "tools" / "util" / "include",
]
SPDLOG_INCLUDE_DIR: pathlib.Path = _package_root / "data" / "spdlog" / "include"
CCCL_INCLUDE_DIRS: list[pathlib.Path] = [
    _package_root / "data" / "cccl" / "cub",
    _package_root / "data" / "cccl" / "libcudacxx" / "include",
    _package_root / "data" / "cccl" / "thrust",
]

import click

from .artifacts import (
    ArtifactPath,
    download_artifacts,
    clear_cubin,
    get_artifacts_status,
)
from .jit import clear_cache_dir, jit_spec_registry
from .jit.cubin_loader import FLASHINFER_CUBINS_REPOSITORY
from .jit.env import FLASHINFER_CACHE_DIR, FLASHINFER_CUBIN_DIR
from .jit.core import current_compilation_context
from .jit.cpp_ext import get_cuda_path, get_cuda_version

# Import __version__ from centralized version module
from .version import __version__


def _download_cubin():
    """Helper function to download cubin"""
    try:
        download_artifacts()
        click.secho("✅ All cubin download tasks completed successfully.", fg="green")
    except Exception as e:
        click.secho(f"❌ Cubin download failed: {e}", fg="red")


def _ensure_modules_registered():
    """Helper function to ensure modules are registered"""
    statuses = jit_spec_registry.get_all_statuses()
    if not statuses:
        click.secho("No modules found. Registering default modules...", fg="yellow")
        try:
            from .aot import register_default_modules

            num_registered = register_default_modules()
            click.secho(f"✅ Registered {num_registered} modules", fg="green")
            statuses = jit_spec_registry.get_all_statuses()
        except Exception as e:
            click.secho(f"❌ Module registration failed: {e}", fg="red")
    return statuses

    """Helper function to download cubin"""
    try:
        download_artifacts()
        click.secho("✅ All cubin download tasks completed successfully.", fg="green")
    except Exception as e:
        click.secho(f"❌ Cubin download failed: {e}", fg="red")


def _ensure_modules_registered():
    """Helper function to ensure modules are registered"""
    statuses = jit_spec_registry.get_all_statuses()
    if not statuses:
        click.secho("No modules found. Registering default modules...", fg="yellow")
        try:
            from .aot import register_default_modules

            num_registered = register_default_modules()
            click.secho(f"✅ Registered {num_registered} modules", fg="green")
            statuses = jit_spec_registry.get_all_statuses()
        except Exception as e:
            click.secho(f"❌ Module registration failed: {e}", fg="red")
    return statuses

- **TensorRT-LLM (trtllm-gen)** - `flashinfer/trtllm_utils.py`, `gen_trtllm_gen_fmha_module()`
- **CUTLASS** - `gen_fmha_cutlass_sm100a_module()`, CUTLASS MLA kernels

**Backend Selection Logic:**
```python
def determine_attention_backend(device, pos_encoding_mode, use_fp16_qk_reductions,
                                 use_custom_mask, dtype_q, dtype_kv, *,
                                 head_dim_qk=None, head_dim_vo=None) -> str:
    # Returns "fa2", "fa3", "cudnn", or "trtllm-gen" based on:
    # - GPU compute capability (sm80, sm90, sm100, sm120)
    # - Data types (fp16, bf16, fp8, nvfp4)

    # - Custom mask requirements
```

- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/trtllm_utils.py` - TensorRT-LLM utilities

---

### Data Flow: Python API -> JIT Gen -> csrc Launcher -> Include Kernel

1. **Python API** (`flashinfer/decode.py`, `flashinfer/prefill.py`)
   - Wrapper class `plan()` analyzes batch structure, calls JIT module's `plan()`
   - Wrapper class `run()` invokes JIT module's `run()` with plan_info

2. **JIT Generation** (`flashinfer/jit/attention/modules.py`)
   - `gen_*_module()` functions render Jinja templates to generate `.cu` and `.inc` files
   - Templates: `*_customize_config.jinja`, `*_kernel_inst.jinja`
   - Compiled via NVRTC to shared library

3. **csrc Launcher** (`csrc/batch_*_jit_binding.cu`)
   - `TVM_FFI_DLL_EXPORT_TYPED_FUNC(plan, ...)` - exported plan function
   - `TVM_FFI_DLL_EXPORT_TYPED_FUNC(run, ...)` - exported run function
   - Calls template-generated kernel functions

4. **Include Kernel** (`include/flashinfer/attention/*.cuh`)
   - `decode.cuh`, `prefill.cuh`, `scheduler.cuh` contain actual CUDA kernels
   - `DISPATCH_*` macros specialize templates for dtypes, head dims, variants

---

This map provides the complete structural overview of FlashInfer's attention subsystem with concrete file paths and their roles in the learning handbook design.</result>

## GEMM Subsystem

- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/attention/_core.py` - Core `BatchAttention` wrapper class with `plan()`/`run()` pattern
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/attention/cute_dsl/__init__.py` - CuTe DSL attention module
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/attention/cute_dsl/fmha.py` - FMHA CuTe DSL implementations

**Main Wrapper Classes:**
- **`BatchAttention`** - Holistic batched attention wrapper (fuses paged-prefill + paged-decode), dispatches per-request based on qo_indptr ranges
- **`BatchAttentionWithAttentionSinkWrapper`** - Extends `BatchPrefillWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`, adds attention sink support
- **`BatchDecodeWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`** (in `decode.py`) - Batch decode with paged KV-cache
- **`BatchPrefillWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`** (in `prefill.py`) - Batch prefill with paged KV-cache
- **`BatchMLAWrapper`** (in `mla/_core.py`) - Multi-head Latent Attention wrapper

**Plan/Run Pattern:**
- `plan(shape_params)` - Analyzes batch structure, creates auxiliary data structures, returns plan_info
- `run(q, kv_cache, ...)` - Executes kernel using cached plan_info, reuses workspace buffers

---

### 2. Main Attention Modules

**`flashinfer/decode.py`** - Batch/single decode attention, MLA decode, cuDNN decode, XQA decode functions
- Entry wrappers: `BatchDecodeWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`, `BatchDecodeMLAWrapper`
- Functions: `single_decode_with_kv_cache()`, `batch_decode_with_kv_cache()`

**`flashinfer/prefill.py`** - Batch/single prefill attention with paged/ragged KV-cache
- Entry wrappers: `BatchPrefillWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`, `BatchPrefillWithRaggedKVCacheWrapper`
- Functions: `single_prefill_with_kv_cache()`, `batch_prefill_with_kv_cache()`

**`flashinfer/cascade.py`** - Cascade attention for recursive state merging
- Functions: `merge_state()`, `merge_states()`, `merge_state_in_place()`
- Module: `gen_cascade_module()`

**`flashinfer/pod.py`** - POD (Position-Output Decoupled) Attention wrapper
- Wrapper: `PODWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`, `BatchPODWithPagedKVCacheWrapper`
- Modules: `gen_pod_module()`, `gen_batch_pod_module()`

**`flashinfer/sparse.py`** - Block sparse attention (BSR to VSA conversion)
- Functions: `block_sparse_attention()`, `variable_block_sparse_attention()`
- Uses custom Q2K indexing patterns

**`flashinfer/xqa.py`** - XQA (Cross-Query Attention) module
- Functions: `xqa()`, `xqa_mla()`
- JIT generators: `gen_xqa_module()`, `gen_xqa_module_mla()`

**`flashinfer/kda_decode.py`** - Key-Driven Attention decode (recurrent KDA)
- Functions: `recurrent_kda()`
- Backend: `flashinfer.kda_kernels.recurrent_kda`

**`flashinfer/mla/`** - Multi-head Latent Attention
- `_core.py` - Core MLA wrapper (`BatchMLAWrapper`), `determine_mla_backend()` dispatch
- `_sparse_mla_sm120.py` - SM120 sparse MLA implementation

### 6. Backend Selection Mechanism

**Backend Dispatch:**
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/utils.py` - `determine_attention_backend()` function (line 483), `backend_requirement` decorator (line 1013)

**Backends:**
- **FlashAttention2 (fa2)** - CUDA cores, sm80+
- **FlashAttention3 (fa3)** - Tensor cores (Hopper sm90), CUTLASS-based
- **cuDNN** - `flashinfer/cudnn/` package with `cudnn_batch_decode_with_kv_cache()`, `cudnn_batch_prefill_with_kv_cache()`
- **TensorRT-LLM (trtllm-gen)** - `flashinfer/trtllm_utils.py`, `gen_trtllm_gen_fmha_module()`
- **CUTLASS** - `gen_fmha_cutlass_sm100a_module()`, CUTLASS MLA kernels

**Backend Selection Logic:**
```python
def determine_attention_backend(device, pos_encoding_mode, use_fp16_qk_reductions,
                                 use_custom_mask, dtype_q, dtype_kv, *,
                                 head_dim_qk=None, head_dim_vo=None) -> str:
    # Returns "fa2", "fa3", "cudnn", or "trtllm-gen" based on:
    # - GPU compute capability (sm80, sm90, sm100, sm120)
    # - Data types (fp16, bf16, fp8, nvfp4)
    # - Head dimensions
    # - Custom mask requirements
```

**Backend Files:**
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/cudnn/decode.py` - cuDNN decode backend
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/cudnn/prefill.py` - cuDNN prefill backend
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/csrc/cudnn_sdpa_kernel_launcher.cu` - cuDNN SDPA launcher
- `/home/runner/work/codeReader/codeReader/work/flashinfer-ai-flashinfer/flashinfer/trtllm_utils.py` - TensorRT-LLM utilities

---

### Data Flow: Python API -> JIT Gen -> csrc Launcher -> Include Kernel

1. **Python API** (`flashinfer/decode.py`, `flashinfer/prefill.py`)
   - Wrapper class `plan()` analyzes batch structure, calls JIT module's `plan()`
   - Wrapper class `run()` invokes JIT module's `run()` with plan_info

2. **JIT Generation** (`flashinfer/jit/attention/modules.py`)
   - `gen_*_module()` functions render Jinja templates to generate `.cu` and `.inc` files
   - Templates: `*_customize_config.jinja`, `*_kernel_inst.jinja`
   - Compiled via NVRTC to shared library

3. **csrc Launcher** (`csrc/batch_*_jit_binding.cu`)
   - `TVM_FFI_DLL_EXPORT_TYPED_FUNC(plan, ...)` - exported plan function
   - `TVM_FFI_DLL_EXPORT_TYPED_FUNC(run, ...)` - exported run function
   - Calls template-generated kernel functions

4. **Include Kernel** (`include/flashinfer/attention/*.cuh`)
   - `decode.cuh`, `prefill.cuh`, `scheduler.cuh` contain actual CUDA kernels
   - `DISPATCH_*` macros specialize templates for dtypes, head dims, variants

---

This map provides the complete structural overview of FlashInfer's attention subsystem with concrete file paths and their roles in the learning handbook design.</result>

content.thinking:
I now have a comprehensive and detailed understanding of the FlashInfer project. I have enough information to design a thorough learning handbook outline. Let me synthesize.

Project: FlashInfer v0.6.14 - a GPU kernel library for LLM serving with JIT compilation.

Key architecture layers:
1. Python package (`flashinfer/`) - high-level APIs
2. JIT system (`flashinfer/jit/`) - JitSpec, codegen, build, env
3. C++ bindings (`csrc/`) - TVM-FFI launchers, jinja templates
4. CUDA kernels (`include/flashinfer/`) - header-only templates
5. Tests (`tests/`), benchmarks (`benchmarks/`), examples (`examples/`)

Subsystems:
- Attention (decode, prefill, MLA, cascade, sparse, POD, XQA, KDA, mamba/SSD, batch_attention, GDN)
- GEMM (bf16, fp8, fp4, grouped, deepgemm, bmm)
- MoE (fused, trtllm, cute_dsl, routing, quantized, bgmv, mono)
- Comm (allreduce, alltoall, mnnvl, nvshmem, all_gather_matmul)
- Other ops: sampling, norm, rope, activation, page, quantization

Cross-cutting:
- JIT compilation (3 layers)
- Backend dispatch (backend_requirement, determine_attention_backend)
- Plan/run pattern
- Autotuning
- Trace / fi_trace
- AOT / precompiled packages (cubin, jit-cache)
- CLI
- @flashinfer_api logging
- TVM-FFI bindings
- Testing framework
- Benchmarking framework (CUPTI)
- Adding new kernels

Now let me design the learning handbook. This is a large project, so 30+ lectures is appropriate. Let me organize into 3 levels with units.

Let me design the structure:

**Unit 1 (beginner): Project Orientation & Getting Started**
- L1: Project overview & positioning (what is FlashInfer, why, features, GPU support)
- L2: Installation & first run (pip install, from source, --recursive, editable, verify, single_decode example)
- L3: Directory structure & codebase layout (include/csrc/flashinfer/tests/benchmarks, framework separation)
- L4: CLI tools & configuration (show-config, list-modules, clear-cache, download-cubin, export-compile-commands)
- L5: First attention kernel in practice (single_decode/single_prefill, basic usage example)

**Unit 2 (beginner-intermediate): The JIT Compilation System**
- L1: What is JIT & why (3-layer overview, cache, no-rebuild dev loop)
- L2: JitSpec & workspace env (env.py paths, JitSpec, gen_jit_spec, build/build_and_load)
- L3: Code generation pattern (gen_*_module, jinja render, copy sources, URI hash)
- L4: Compilation context & CUDA archs (CompilationContext, TARGET_CUDA_ARCHS, supported versions)
- L5: Module caching & cache invalidation (@functools.cache, file-level cache, URI, env vars)

**Unit 3 (intermediate): Attention Fundamentals**
- L1: Attention in LLM serving (decode vs prefill vs append, why paged KV)
- L2: Paged KV Cache layout (page.py, TensorLayout NHD/HND, append_paged_kv_cache, page table)
- L3: BatchDecodeWithPagedKVCacheWrapper plan/run (decode.py wrapper, workspace, plan_info)
- L4: BatchPrefillWithPagedKVCacheWrapper plan/run (prefill.py)
- L5: Backend selection (determine_attention_backend, fa2/fa3/cudnn/trtllm, utils.py, backend_requirement)

**Unit 4 (intermediate): Advanced Attention Variants**
- L1: MLA - Multi-Latent Attention (mla/_core.py, BatchMLAWrapper, DeepSeek)
- L2: Cascade attention - shared prefixes (cascade.py, merge_state)
- L3: Sparse & block-sparse attention (sparse.py, BSR->VSA)
- L4: POD attention - fused prefill+decode mixed batching (pod.py, batch_pod)
- L5: BatchAttention - holistic mixed batching (attention/_core.py)
- L6: Custom attention variants (customize_config, test_jit_example)

**Unit 5 (intermediate): GEMM & Low-Precision Compute**
- L1: GEMM landscape in FlashInfer (gemm/__init__, gemm_base.py, mm_bf16/fp8/fp4)
- L2: FP8 GEMM (per-tensor & groupwise, fp8_gemm_cutlass)
- L3: FP4 GEMM (nvfp4/mxfp4, Blackwell, fp4_gemm_cutlass)
- L4: Grouped GEMM (grouped_mm/, LoRA/multi-expert, group_gemm.cuh)
- L5: Quantization ops (fp8_quantization, fp4_quantization, quantize/dequantize)

**Unit 6 (intermediate-advanced): Mixture of Experts (MoE)**
- L1: MoE fundamentals & routing (fused_moe/__init__, MoEConfig, routing modes)
- L2: Fused MoE backends (cutlass_fused_moe, trtllm_bf16/fp8/fp4_moe, sm-specific)
- L3: Routing methods (fused_routing_dsv3 DeepSeek-V3, Llama-4, top-k)
- L4: Quantized MoE & MoELayer dispatcher (FP8/FP4, layer.py, runners)

**Unit 7 (intermediate): Other Core Operators**
- L1: Sampling & decoding (sampling.py, top-k/p/min-p, sorting-free, speculative)
- L2: Normalization (rmsnorm, layernorm, fused_add_rmsnorm, norm/__init__)
- L3: RoPE - rotary position embedding (rope.py, LLaMA)
- L4: Activations (activation.py, silu/gelu_and_mul, fused gating)
- L5: Mamba/SSD state-space models (mamba/, selective_state_update, ssd_combined)
- L6: GDN & KDA newer kernels (gdn_decode, kda_decode - optional/advanced)

**Unit 8 (advanced): Multi-GPU Communication**
- L1: Communication overview & AllReduce (comm/__init__, allreduce_fusion, trtllm_ar strategies)
- L2: Custom AllReduce backends (vllm_ar, trtllm_ar, MIN_LATENCY/ONESHOT/TWOSHOT)
- L3: AlltoAll & multi-node (moe_a2a, dcp_alltoall, MNNVL, nvshmem)
- L4: AllGather-Matmul fusion (all_gather_matmul/, cutile vs triton)

**Unit 9 (advanced): Extending FlashInfer**
- L1: Adding a new CUDA kernel - full pipeline (skill, include kernel -> csrc launcher -> binding -> jit gen -> python api -> aot -> export)
- L2: TVM-FFI bindings & cross-language ABI (TVM_FFI_DLL_EXPORT_TYPED_FUNC, jit_binding.cu)
- L3: Jinja templates & dispatch macros (customize_config.jinja, DISPATCH_DTYPE, kernel_inst)
- L4: AOT compilation & precompiled packages (aot.py, register_default_modules, cubin/jit-cache)
- L5: Trace system & fi_trace (trace/templates, TraceTemplate, @flashinfer_api trace, fi_trace.py)

**Unit 10 (advanced): Engineering, Performance & Operations**
- L1: API logging & debugging (@flashinfer_api, api_logging, FLASHINFER_LOGLEVEL)
- L2: Autotuning (autotuner.py, FLASHINFER_AUTOTUNER_LOAD_FROM_FILE)
- L3: Benchmarking framework (benchmarks/flashinfer_benchmark.py, CUPTI, bench_gpu_time)
- L4: Testing framework & arch gating (tests/, conftest, is_smXX_supported, backend_requirement checks)
- L5: Environment variables reference (FLASHINFER_* full reference)

Hmm, that's about 50 lectures which might be too many. Let me consolidate. The planner says "大型项目可以拆成 30 篇以上讲义". FlashInfer is a large project. 35-45 is reasonable. But let me make sure each is a focused single topic and not over-split.

Let me reconsider and aim for ~40 lectures across 10 units, which is solid for a large project. Actually, let me trim slightly to keep quality high. Let me reconsider the GDN/KDA which are very niche - I'll fold KDA into "newer experimental kernels" or merge. Actually GDN (gated delta net) and KDA (key-driven attention) are real subsystems with their own dirs, worth a mention but I can combine.

Let me reconsider Unit 7 - maybe combine some. And Unit 10 L5 (env vars) can be folded into JIT lectures.

Let me finalize a clean structure. I'll aim for about 40 lectures. Let me reconsider the dependency graph and make it coherent.

Actually, let me be a bit more disciplined. I'll structure:

Unit 1: Orientation (beginner) — 5 lectures
Unit 2: JIT System (beginner→intermediate) — 5 lectures
Unit 3: Attention Foundations (intermediate) — 5 lectures
Unit 4: Advanced Attention (intermediate→advanced) — 5 lectures
Unit 5: GEMM & Quantization (intermediate) — 5 lectures
Unit 6: MoE (intermediate→advanced) — 4 lectures
Unit 7: Core Operators (intermediate) — 5 lectures
Unit 8: Multi-GPU Comm (advanced) — 4 lectures
Unit 9: Extending FlashInfer (advanced) — 5 lectures
Unit 10: Performance & Ops (advanced) — 4 lectures

Total: 47. That's a bit high. Let me trim to ~42. 

Actually, for a project this large with this many subsystems, 40+ is justified and the planner explicitly allows 30+ for large projects. But let me make each genuinely independent and valuable. Let me merge a few:

- Merge Unit 7's GDN/KDA into a single "experimental/newer kernels" lecture.
- Merge env vars into JIT unit (already have caching). Actually env vars reference is valuable as standalone advanced reference. I'll keep but make it part of Unit 10.
